warnings: mode=adiabatic: each oxidation state was geometry-optimized separately and the potential uses ΔE between the relaxed geometries (includes reorganization). It does NOT include ZPE/thermal/entropy (ΔE used in place of ΔG); use mode=freq for a true ΔG.

Solvation is implicit/continuum only (or gas phase) — no explicit solvent shell and no Born–Haber solvation cycle. Semi-empirical redox potentials are screening-grade (≈±0.3–0.5 V); even DFT here omits the explicit-solvation correction. Treat as a screening estimate.

E°(vs SHE) uses E_abs(SHE) = 4.281 V. The absolute SHE potential is convention-dependent (literature ≈4.28–4.44 V); this is a systematic offset on the reported potential. Override with --e-abs-she to match your reference convention.

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal mol⁻¹ — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: Standard redox potential E° (0 → −1 couple) of benzoquinone in water, referenced to the standard hydrogen electrode (SHE).

Level of theory: GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding (Bannwarth–Grimme), as implemented in the xtb backend of chemkit.

Geometry provenance: adiabatic protocol (redox) — the oxidized (charge 0, multiplicity 1) and reduced (charge −1, multiplicity 2) states were each fully geometry-optimized at GFN2-xTB (converged optimizations; single-point energies taken at these relaxed geometries). This is a ΔE(adiabatic) calculation, i.e. no vibrational or thermal corrections.

Solvent / environment: ALPB implicit water (continuum solvation) as provided by xtb. No explicit solvent molecules included.

Charge and multiplicity:
 • Oxidized state: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet)
 • Reduced state: charge −1, multiplicity 2 (doublet)

Software: xtb via chemkit redox-potential skill (cli invocation: `chemkit redox --method xtb --solvent water --ref SHE --ox-charge 0 --red-charge -1 --ox-mult 1 --red-mult 2 benzoquinone.xyz`); see live log at /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/redox-potential-validation/benzoquinone/20260625-115403_benzoquinone_redox_potential/redox_20260625-115412.out (written during execution).

Convergence & caveats: Both oxidation states converged. Density-fitted GFN2-xTB used. Warnings from engine are listed above and should be considered when interpreting the result.

Headline result
Standard redox potential (E° vs SHE) = 4.9045828 V

This is a computed screening-level estimate; absolute accuracy is typically ±0.3–0.5 V for GFN2-xTB in implicit solvent.
